# Wazir

Wazir is a host-native engineering OS kit that gives AI coding agents (Claude, Codex, Gemini, Cursor) canonical roles, phased workflows, callable skills, and quality gates -- so you drop a task in and get production-ready code out.

## Install

```bash
npm install -g @wazir-dev/cli
```

Then in Claude Code:

```
/plugin marketplace add MohamedAbdallah-14/Wazir
/plugin install wazir
```

## Pipeline

14 phases, 3 approval gates. The agent cannot skip, shortcut, or rubber-stamp its way through.

```
clarify -> discover -> specify -> spec-challenge (GATE)
  -> author -> design -> design-review (GATE)
  -> plan -> plan-review (GATE)
  -> execute -> verify -> review -> learn -> prepare-next
```

Gates reject work back to the authoring phase until quality passes.

## Key Concepts

- 14 phases: Each phase has a dedicated role. The reviewer is never the author.
- 10 roles: clarifier, researcher, specifier, content-author, designer, planner, executor, verifier, reviewer, learner.
- 3 approval gates: spec-challenge, design-review, plan-review.
- Composition engine: Deterministic function (not LLM) that assembles task-specific agent prompts from 270 expertise modules across 13 domains.
- Enforcement: Hook guardrails + schema validation target 99%+ per-step compliance. Prompt-only compliance caps at 40-50%.
- Fresh agents: Every agent is born, does one job, and dies. No session continuity. Context rot is worse than one extra spawn.
- TDD: Mandatory. Verification requires proof artifacts with fresh command output.
- Interaction modes: auto (zero human stops), guided (default, 2 pause points + boundary gates), interactive (stops between every sub-phase).

## Agent Policy

- Only the executor role gets Edit capability.
- The controller (orchestrator) is dispatch-only: read, shell, search, skills, agents -- no write/edit.
- Each dispatched agent gets a fresh context window, only its role contract, and relevant expertise modules.
- Tool restrictions enforced by YAML frontmatter in .claude/agents/.

## Where to Find Things

```
roles/           10 canonical role contracts
workflows/       14 phase entrypoints
skills/          30 callable procedures (wz: namespace)
hooks/           7 guardrail definitions
expertise/       270 knowledge modules (13 domains)
templates/       Artifact formats and phase checklists
schemas/         Validation schemas (manifest, hooks, artifacts)
exports/         Generated host packages (claude, codex, gemini, cursor)
tooling/         CLI source (validation, indexing, recall, capture)
docs/concepts/   Architecture, roles, composition engine
docs/reference/  Lookup tables, review dimensions
docs/research/   123 research files (14 categories, 47+ papers)
docs/vision/     Pipeline source of truth (locked)
```

## Key Files

- `wazir.manifest.yaml` -- project manifest and schema
- `docs/concepts/architecture.md` -- system architecture
- `docs/concepts/roles-and-workflows.md` -- role and phase concepts
- `docs/reference/roles-reference.md` -- role and workflow lookup tables
- `expertise/composition-map.yaml` -- agent policy and composition rules
- `CLAUDE.md` -- project instructions for Claude
- `AGENTS.md` -- agent dispatch table

## Research Basis

Every pipeline design decision is grounded in empirical research (47+ papers). Key constraints: 39% multi-turn degradation, 64.5% self-correction blind spot, 40-50% prompt-only compliance ceiling. Full index: docs/research/INDEX.md.

## Full Documentation

See llms-full.txt for comprehensive context including README, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, architecture, and roles reference.
